creator: freebsd@dev.thsi.be
text:
(In reply to Guido Falsi from comment #1)

Hi Guido,

Thank you for the feedback, the review and encouragement.

> updating ocaml is a complex task, as I myself discovered when I updated to 4.08.1

I'm hoping to be able bring the 4.14.1 update that I have locally. Thank your for having done the 4.08 step, including the required wider ecosystem updates, as it makes this update easier. I think that I understand what it takes, as I had already done a quick and dirty 4.13 upgrade 2 years ago, and now the 4.14.1 update with most if not all application ported already. But I might under or over estimate the difficulty of getting all the patches to the various ports, and the importance (or not) of keeping application versions around (looking at Coq here...). I appreciate your help and input already, and surely on later submissions.

> - You moved PORTNAME after PORTVERSION, leaving an empty line. You should correct this, as it is against port rules.

Fixed in new patch.

> - You have removed newlines from the last lines of files.

I can't seem to find that problem in my submission(s). Could it be that some issue or mixing-up occured on your side ? Or would you be kind to be very specific, as I may be missing an otherwise obvious thing :/


> it would really help if you provided conforming patches, which makes committers work much easier.

Noted. I'd like too to minize the commiters' burden of my submissions too. Those are still my first contributions to the project, they will improve with further experience, or at least I hope so :)

Thanks!
